mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 65% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term positive repricing of S&P 500 equities within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 70% of periods following positive INDPRO revisions, or the signal loses predictive power during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses corporate margins and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are both elevated, with a hit rate below 40% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports aggregate consumption, leading to higher corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth surpassing 2.5 percent annualized confirms robust economic expansion, reinforcing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 65% of instances when GDPC1 crosses the 2.5% threshold, invalidating the growth-to-equity transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total payrolls indicate synchronized supply-side and labor market strength, triggering immediate positive momentum in S&P 500 equities over the following week.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields a negative or zero alpha over 5-day horizons in more than 60% of observations, or the joint signal underperforms single-series baselines.
